notes: NI Abbey Road 70s Drummer — vintage 1970s drum kit recorded at Abbey Road Studios. Based on a Ludwig Vistalite-style acrylic kit — the sound of classic rock, funk, and hard rock. Character: big, fat, open drum sound with strong fundamentals; kick has significant low-end weight, snare is thick and resonant with clear crack, toms ring out with long sustains. The 70s saw more close-mic recording but still with significant room use — balance QC1–2 for presence and QC4 for the big room sound. Suits classic rock, funk, hard rock, prog, and vintage-feel productions. The tom level (CC 26) is particularly important on this kit — the 70s tom sound is huge. Compression (CC 28) can push towards the dense classic rock wall-of-sound character.

Quick Control 1.description: Kick Close Mic Level — weighty 70s kick with strong low-end fundamental

Quick Control 2.description: Snare Top Mic Level — thick, resonant 70s snare crack

Quick Control 3.description: Overhead Mic Level — wide overhead capture with open cymbals

Quick Control 4.description: Room Mic Level — big Abbey Road room ambience; key to the 70s large-room drum sound

Quick Control 5.description: Kick Sub Level — substantial low-end body; this kit has more sub than earlier eras

Quick Control 6.description: Snare Bottom Mic — snare wire character and buzz

Quick Control 8.description: Reverb/Room Blend — close vs room ratio

Room Level.description: Room microphone level — push for the signature 70s big room sound

Tom Level.description: Tom drums overall level — particularly important on this kit; 70s toms are a defining feature

Compression.description: Bus compression — higher values approach classic rock wall-of-sound density
